name: Software Engineering Intern
summary: Builds and ships features in a real codebase alongside engineers — usually web, mobile, or backend services.
jargonNote: This means writing the actual code that makes an app or website work — not designing how it looks, but building how it runs.
requiredSkills: one mainstream language (Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, C++, or Go)
reading and modifying an existing codebase
debugging with a debugger or logs
explaining a technical decision in writing
niceToHaveSkills: one shipped side-project with a public repo
a web framework (React, Next.js, or similar)
typicalTimeline: 12–18 months from zero to first SWE internship
signalsRecruitersWant: a GitHub with real commits, not just clones
one project they can describe end-to-end in 90 seconds
a LeetCode/CSES habit, even if early
a referral or a club/team project on the resume

name: Product Manager Intern
summary: Sits between users, design, and engineering — defines what to build, why, and in what order.
jargonNote: A PM figures out what the team should build next and why — think of it as being the translator between users, designers, and engineers.
requiredSkills: running a meeting with an agenda
niceToHaveSkills: shipped a small product (even a class project) end-to-end
ran a club initiative with metrics
wrote a public teardown or product analysis
typicalTimeline: 18–24 months — most PM interns first do SWE, design, or APM-feeder roles
signalsRecruitersWant: evidence of leading something cross-functional
a written artifact (PRD, teardown, case study)
a story that ties a decision to a measurable outcome
comfort speaking to a room of strangers

name: Data Analyst Intern
summary: Pulls data, cleans it, and turns it into a chart, dashboard, or recommendation a non-technical team can act on.
jargonNote: This role pulls numbers out of databases, turns them into charts, and tells the team what the data actually means.
requiredSkills: Excel or Google Sheets to an intermediate level
one BI tool (Tableau, Looker, or Power BI) — even self-taught
Python or R for data wrangling (pandas/tidyverse)
writing a one-paragraph insight from a chart
presenting a finding to a non-technical audience
niceToHaveSkills: experience with a real (not toy) dataset
domain knowledge in finance, marketing, or ops
typicalTimeline: 9–15 months — one of the most accessible first internships
signalsRecruitersWant: a portfolio link with at least one analysis
ability to describe a query they wrote
evidence of curiosity (a Kaggle, a club analysis, a personal project)
clean writing in a cover letter or LinkedIn About section

name: Data Scientist / ML Intern
summary: Builds models or rigorous experiments that change a product or business decision.
jargonNote: This is building AI models — teaching a computer to make predictions or decisions based on data. Needs more math than a data analyst role.
requiredSkills: supervised learning basics (regression, classification, evaluation metrics)
reading a research paper enough to summarize it
framing a business question as a model task
niceToHaveSkills: an end-to-end ML project (data → model → write-up)
cloud compute experience (Colab, Lightning, or AWS)
a Kaggle entry or blog post
typicalTimeline: 18–30 months — usually requires upper-division coursework or research
signalsRecruitersWant: one ML project beyond a tutorial
ability to defend a modeling choice ('why this loss?')
research or coursework that goes deeper than the syllabus
a writeup that frames the problem before the model

name: UX Research Intern
summary: Designs and runs studies — interviews, surveys, usability tests — that change what the team builds.
jargonNote: This role talks to real users, runs studies, and reports back on what people actually need — so designers and PMs don't have to guess.
requiredSkills: writing a research plan with a clear question
presenting findings to designers and PMs
reading a research paper or industry report
niceToHaveSkills: qualitative coding tools (Dovetail, Marvin) or even just Notion
ran a study in a class or club
typicalTimeline: 15–24 months — often follows a research-method or psych course
signalsRecruitersWant: a case study describing question → method → finding → impact
evidence of empathy in writing
experience presenting research to a non-research audience
comfort with ambiguity

name: Product Design Intern
summary: Designs interfaces and flows for a product team — often pairing closely with PMs and engineers.
jargonNote: This is designing how an app or website looks and works — the buttons, layouts, and flows that users actually interact with.
requiredSkills: writing UI copy that doesn't get in the way
describing a design decision in plain language
designing for at least mobile and desktop
niceToHaveSkills: a public portfolio with 2–3 case studies
typicalTimeline: 12–24 months — portfolio-driven; speed depends on case-study quality
signalsRecruitersWant: a portfolio that tells the story, not just shows the screens
a process artifact (sketches, alternatives considered)
evidence of working with non-designers
taste — referenced products and inspirations

name: Finance Analyst Intern
summary: Builds models, decks, and analyses for investment banking, corporate finance, or asset management roles.
jargonNote: This role builds spreadsheet models, reads company financial reports, and helps teams figure out if a deal or investment makes sense.
requiredSkills: the three financial statements and how they connect
DCF and comparable-company concepts at a high level
PowerPoint / Google Slides for executive comms
comfort with quantitative reasoning under time pressure
niceToHaveSkills: a stock pitch or investment memo (even for a class)
involvement in a finance club or fund
a relevant certification in progress (CFA Level 1, etc.)
typicalTimeline: 12–24 months — recruiting starts very early in finance, often sophomore fall
signalsRecruitersWant: GPA and relevant coursework
a deal or stock pitch they can talk through
a finance-club leadership role
polish — resume, email, handshake, eye contact

name: Marketing Intern
summary: Helps run campaigns, content, social, or growth experiments — often analytical and creative at once.
jargonNote: This is getting people to notice and care about a product — through social media, campaigns, ads, or content.
requiredSkills: writing a tight headline and a paragraph that doesn't waste a reader's time
social media platform conventions (LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok)
running a small experiment with a clear metric
Canva or Figma for quick visuals
presenting results to a non-marketing team
niceToHaveSkills: a personal brand or content channel with engagement
ran marketing for a campus org or event
typicalTimeline: 9–15 months — accessible if you can show a portfolio or campaign
signalsRecruitersWant: a body of work — posts, campaigns, or events with metrics
a clear voice in writing
evidence of running a thing, not just helping with it
comfort being on camera or in front of a room

name: Consulting Analyst Intern
summary: Helps client teams structure problems, gather evidence, and recommend a path — usually in slides.
jargonNote: Consultants help outside companies solve business problems — you come in, figure out what's wrong, and present a recommendation in slides.
requiredSkills: structuring a vague problem into MECE buckets
summarizing a 30-minute interview into a one-pager
niceToHaveSkills: consulting club involvement and case practice
a project that solved a real problem for an external client
exposure to SQL or basic data analysis
a leadership role with measurable impact
typicalTimeline: 15–24 months — competitive; case prep is a multi-month effort
signalsRecruitersWant: GPA and case-interview readiness
leadership experience with a story arc
polish in conversation and writing
diverse interests outside the major

name: Hardware / Embedded Engineering Intern
summary: Designs or programs physical systems — PCBs, microcontrollers, robotics, or signal processing.
jargonNote: This is building and programming physical things — circuit boards, robots, sensors — not just software.
requiredSkills: C or C++ on a microcontroller (Arduino, STM32, ESP32, etc.)
reading a schematic and a datasheet
circuit fundamentals (Ohm's law, RC circuits, op-amps)
niceToHaveSkills: an embedded project on a public repo
typicalTimeline: 15–24 months — project-portfolio driven, similar to SWE
signalsRecruitersWant: a project they can show photos and a writeup of
a club or competition team (robotics, IEEE, hardware hackathons)
comfort talking about failure modes
a willingness to break things on purpose

name: Bioengineering / Wet-Lab Research Intern
summary: Joins a lab and contributes to experiments — pipettes, protocols, data, and the occasional poster.
jargonNote: This means working in a science lab, running experiments, and collecting data — usually the first step toward medicine, pharma, or academic research.
requiredSkills: lab safety and good lab notebook habits
basic wet-lab techniques (pipetting, dilutions, sterile technique)
asking a PI or grad student a focused question
niceToHaveSkills: Python or R for bio data
exposure to specific techniques (PCR, Western blot, microscopy, CRISPR)
co-author on a poster or paper
a relevant scholarship or program (URH, McNair, Amgen, etc.)
typicalTimeline: 6–12 months to first lab placement; 18–24 months to industry internship
signalsRecruitersWant: a faculty or postdoc reference
a focused interest area (not 'all of biology')
consistency — months in the same lab beats weeks in three
the ability to explain your project to a non-bio person

name: DevOps / SRE Intern
summary: Keeps services reliable and developer experience fast — CI/CD, infra, observability, and incidents.
jargonNote: This role keeps the servers and tools that run software working reliably — think of it as the plumbing that makes apps stay online.
requiredSkills: git and a CI tool (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar)
basic cloud literacy (AWS, GCP, or Azure free tier)
niceToHaveSkills: an open-source PR to a tooling project
typicalTimeline: 15–24 months — usually after a SWE-style first internship or a strong systems class
signalsRecruitersWant: a project that touches deploy, not just code
a homelab, club infra, or contribution to a tool you use
calm writing about a thing that broke
curiosity about how the box works

name: Healthcare Research Intern
summary: Supports medical or public-health research teams with study coordination, data cleanup, patient-facing materials, or literature review.
jargonNote: This means helping run medical studies or supporting hospital teams with data and documentation — a common first step toward medicine or public health.
requiredSkills: reading clinical or public-health research at a beginner level
following a protocol without skipping steps
niceToHaveSkills: biology, psychology, public health, or sociology coursework
volunteer experience with patients or community programs
typicalTimeline: 6–18 months — often starts with volunteering, coursework, or a research assistant role
signalsRecruitersWant: reliability with sensitive information
a clear reason for caring about health work
evidence of careful documentation
experience working with people who are under stress

name: Nonprofit / Community Program Intern
summary: Helps a mission-driven organization run events, support volunteers, write materials, collect data, or serve a specific community.
jargonNote: This is working for an organization whose goal is to help people, not make profit — you might run events, manage volunteers, or write grant proposals.
requiredSkills: turning messy needs into a simple plan
reflecting on impact, not just effort
typicalTimeline: 3–12 months — one of the fastest paths from campus involvement to real responsibility
signalsRecruitersWant: evidence that people trusted you with responsibility
a story about helping a specific community
clear writing samples
follow-through across multiple weeks, not one event

name: Education / Tutoring Intern
summary: Works directly with students through tutoring, mentoring, classroom support, curriculum help, or college-access programming.
jargonNote: This means working directly with students — tutoring, mentoring, or helping run after-school or college-access programs.
requiredSkills: adapting when a student is confused
niceToHaveSkills: coursework in education, psychology, or the subject being taught
typicalTimeline: 3–9 months — tutoring and mentoring roles are often accessible early
signalsRecruitersWant: proof that students came back or improved
a calm story about helping someone struggle through a concept
consistent weekly commitment
comfort explaining without judging

name: Bilingual Outreach Intern
summary: Uses language and cultural fluency to help organizations reach families, students, patients, or community members who are often missed.
jargonNote: This role uses your second language as a professional skill — translating, connecting communities to services, and building trust across language barriers.
requiredSkills: speaking and writing clearly in two languages
niceToHaveSkills: experience helping family or community members navigate systems
public health, education, or nonprofit interest
simple flyer or social media design
typicalTimeline: 3–12 months — lived language experience can become a professional signal quickly
signalsRecruitersWant: trust-building across cultures or languages
evidence of reliability with sensitive conversations
clear examples of translating or explaining complex information
community commitment beyond a single event
